title: MovenRun documentation
description: MovenRun is an onchain movement network on Base: move in the real world, verifiably own the territory you cover, and share in a community-governed economy — with an experience anyone can use, no crypto knowledge required.
keywords: movenrun documentation movenrun is an onchain movement network. you move through the real world — walking, running, cycling — and the ground you cover becomes territory you verifiably own, trade, and govern. it runs on base, and it is built so anyone can play in seconds, with no seed phrases, no gas, and no crypto experience required. in one paragraph. every session turns real movement into a cryptographic proof of activity that settles onchain. that proof lets you capture map zones, hold them as zone deeds you truly own, and earn $move — the asset that powers the whole economy. clubs contest districts, cities run seasons, and the network itself is designed to be owned and governed by the people who move on it. why movenrun is different you own what you cover territory isn't a score in a database — it's an onchain asset with your name on it. movement you can prove real activity becomes a

title: Executive summary
description: MovenRun in brief: the opportunity, the product, the onchain architecture, the token and governance model, and the roadmap to a fully community-owned movement network.
keywords: executive summary movenrun makes the physical world ownable. it turns everyday movement into verifiable onchain territory — a global game of capture, ownership, and competition, wrapped in an experience anyone can pick up instantly. the opportunity billions of people move every day, and that movement is one of the largest sources of untapped, real-world activity in the world. fitness apps measure it and forget it. earlier "move" tokens tied it to speculation and collapsed. movenrun takes a different path: it gives movement a durable reason to matter by converting it into ownership — territory you hold, build on, and control — inside an economy designed to last rather than to pump. the product you open movenrun and start moving. your route lights up a hexagonal map of the real world; the zones you cross become yours to capture. hold a zone and it becomes a zone deed — an onchain asset you

title: MovenRun Whitepaper
description: The MovenRun whitepaper: the problem, the product thesis, the territory protocol, verifiable movement, the onchain architecture on Base, the $MOVE economy, decentralized governance, and the roadmap.
keywords: movenrun whitepaper an onchain movement network on base · movenrun documentation this whitepaper describes the movenrun protocol, product, and economy as designed. movenrun is a development-stage product; this document is informational and forward-looking, and the design may evolve. nothing here is financial advice or a promise of returns. see legal & disclaimers . 1. introduction movenrun is an onchain network that turns real-world movement into ownable digital territory. players move through their city and claim the ground they cover on a global hexagonal map; the territory they hold is recorded onchain as an asset they own, defend, trade, and build on. a native token, $move, powers the economy, and the network is designed to be governed by its community. critically, the entire experience is built to feel like a mainstream mobile game — the blockchain underneath is invisible to any

title: Vision
description: MovenRun's mission: turn the entire world into an ownable movement game, powered by an onchain economy that feels effortless for everyone — crypto-native or not.
keywords: vision turn the whole world into an ownable game — and make owning a piece of it as easy as going for a walk. mission movenrun's mission is to give real-world movement lasting value by making it the way you earn, own, and govern territory. we believe the physical world should be a shared game board that anyone can play on and truly own a piece of — not a data set that platforms harvest, and not a speculative scheme that burns the people it attracts. what we're building an onchain movement network where the ground you cover becomes yours. move to capture zones; hold them as assets; combine them into districts; compete for cities; and help govern the network you're building. it is a game, an economy, and a piece of digital geography all at once — settled onchain on base and open to everyone. who it's for everyday movers who want their walks, runs, and rides to build something that lasts. e

title: The core loop
description: Move, Capture, Own, Grow — how MovenRun turns every walk, run, and ride into verifiable territory you truly own and can build on.
keywords: the core loop move → capture → own → grow. four verbs that turn a walk into an asset — and an asset into a citywide presence. a closed loop where every session makes the next one matter more. move walk, run, or cycle — all three are first-class. your session records a route and turns it into a verifiable proof of movement . there are no minimum speeds and nothing to stare at mid-session; the game rewards showing up and covering ground. capture your route intersects zones on a global hex map, and finishing a session claims them. capture is the moment a walk becomes a foothold on the map — the first step from moving to owning. own captured zones become zone deeds : onchain assets held under your keys. ownership is the heart of movenrun — a deed is yours to keep, strengthen, defend against challengers, and transfer. this is what separates movenrun from every scoreboard: the map remembers yo

title: Movement & proof of activity
description: How a MovenRun session works: real-world GPS movement becomes a cryptographic proof of activity that settles onchain and drives everything you earn.
keywords: movement & proof of activity the session is the heart of movenrun — and it's also where trust begins. real movement becomes a proof the whole economy can rely on. a session, start to finish tap to start and you're moving within seconds — no setup, no wallet prompt. the route draws live as you go, with distance, time, and pace kept simple and glanceable so your eyes stay on your surroundings. pause and resume whenever you need. finishing produces a clean summary and the capture moment that turns the outing into territory. one session, end to end. it loops back on returning — tomorrow's walk starts here. why movement is proven because territory is a real asset, the game can't take movement on faith. every session is validated for physical plausibility — realistic speed, sane accuracy, continuous motion — and reduced to a deterministic proof that the network's oracle attests. that proof

title: Territory & ownership
description: How the world becomes an ownable hexagonal map: capture zones by moving, hold and fortify them, group them into districts, and own them as onchain assets.
keywords: territory & ownership the world is a hexagonal map, and every hex is a piece of property waiting to be claimed. move to capture it; hold it to own it; fortify it to keep it. what a zone is a zone is one hexagonal cell of the real world — the atom of movenrun territory. capture one and it becomes a zone deed , an onchain asset you hold under your own keys. zones are consistent for everyone: two players standing on the same corner are in the same zone, everywhere on earth. the grid comes from the open h3 system — explained simply in territory computation . capturing and holding finishing a session claims the zones your route crossed. holdings persist onchain and appear across your map, your profile, and each zone's detail view. the more you return, the stronger your claim. the life of a zone territory strengthens when you return and comes under pressure when you don't. captured → held

title: Progression & rewards
description: XP, levels, streaks, movement points, collections, seasons, and district mastery — a deep progression system backed by a real onchain economy.
keywords: progression & rewards movenrun rewards you two ways at once: a deep in-game progression you can feel every session, and a real onchain economy you actually own. levels, xp, and streaks xp and levels track your overall journey and unlock capability as you climb. streaks reward consistency — the single strongest driver of progress in movenrun. show up, and everything compounds. daily and seasonal objectives give each day and each season a clear focus and payoff. movement points and $move every session earns movement points that reflect your activity. as the onchain economy comes online, verified movement mints $move — the network's asset — within a capped schedule, and your territory earns in-game yield while you actively hold it. progression and ownership reinforce each other: the more consistently you move, the more you hold and the more your holdings do for you. collections, mastery

title: Clubs, cities & competition
description: Clubs, crew missions, district control, city wars, and sponsored zones — the social and competitive layer that turns a city into a living contest.
keywords: clubs, cities & competition alone you hold a neighborhood. together you take a city. clubs turn movenrun into a living, citywide contest. clubs and crews join a club and your movement contributes to a shared cause: collective territory, combined district control, and a place on the club leaderboard. a club territory view ties your zones, districts, and rivalries into a single strategic picture, and crew missions turn that picture into weekly team goals worth chasing together. district control and city wars the city layer is where clubs collide. districts are the prize; city wars are season-long battles to control them. your daily movement is a move in a much larger game — the walk that fortifies one zone can be the difference in who holds a district when the season settles. zones build into districts; clubs contest districts; seasons crown cities — then it begins again. rivals and pr

title: Digital ownership
description: Zone Deeds turn the places you move through into assets you genuinely own onchain — portable, provable, and yours, with no wallet friction to get started.
keywords: digital ownership in most games, "your" territory is a row in someone else's database. in movenrun, it's yours — an asset you hold onchain, provably, portably, permanently. what you own every captured zone can become a zone deed : a unique onchain asset representing that piece of the map, held under your own keys. a deed isn't a badge the game grants and can revoke — it's property you control. you can hold it, strengthen it, defend it, and transfer it, and its history travels with it. a zone deed binds a place on the map to an onchain asset you truly own. why ownership changes the game it persists. your territory is still yours tomorrow, next season, next year. it compounds. active deeds earn standing and in-game yield, and combine into districts and city power. it's portable. ownership lives onchain, under your keys — not locked inside one app's account. it's provable. anyone can verify

title: Movement for everyone
description: MovenRun rewards consistency over intensity and is designed for every fitness level and every user — including people who have never touched crypto.
headings: Accessible to everyone — including non-crypto users
keywords: movement for everyone movenrun is tuned so a 20-minute walk is a first-class move — because a network that only rewards athletes never changes how people actually live. every level, every mode walking, running, and cycling are equal — no mode is "better," and there are no minimum speeds. ownership grows from returning , not from raw pace or distance, so consistency beats intensity. daily goals fit into a lunch break; the strongest players are the most regular ones. accessible to everyone — including non-crypto users movenrun's biggest inclusivity decision is technical: by hiding wallets, gas, and jargon, it opens onchain ownership to people who would never make it past a typical crypto sign-up. no prior knowledge, no purchase, no setup — just movement. that's how a network reaches beyond the already-converted and becomes something anyone can join. see onchain, made invisible . designed t

title: Privacy & your data
description: How MovenRun handles location: privacy-first movement, minimal data, verifiable proofs instead of raw trails, and clear user control.
keywords: privacy & your data a movement network runs on location — which is exactly why movenrun is built to prove your activity without hoarding your life. privacy-first by design movenrun's design goal is simple: keep as little sensitive data as possible, and turn movement into proofs rather than permanent trails. what the network needs is confidence that you really moved — not a searchable history of everywhere you've been. verification is built around that principle. what the network uses your route, to play. location is read during an active session to draw your route and determine which zones you captured. proofs, to settle. sessions are reduced to compact, deterministic proofs of movement; it's the proof — not a raw trail — that drives onchain capture. your holdings, onchain. deeds and balances live onchain under your keys, as ownership records — not as a profile of your movements. you

title: Architecture
description: The MovenRun system: a consumer app, a movement-verification layer, and an onchain protocol on Base — designed so real-world activity settles into onchain ownership.
keywords: architecture movenrun is three layers working as one: an effortless app, a verification layer that proves movement, and an onchain protocol on base that settles ownership and value. solid, real-world movement flows up into onchain ownership on base. the three layers what each layer does layer role experience the mobile app and its embedded wallet: onboarding, movement sessions, the territory map, clubs, and the game — with all crypto handled invisibly. verification turns a recorded route into a validated, deterministic proof of movement and attests it via the oracle, so onchain actions can trust real-world activity. protocol the onchain contracts on base: the $move token, zone deeds, challenges, seasons, the staking vault, and governance — the system of record for ownership and value. how activity becomes ownership a player moves; the app records the route and requests a proof. the verif

title: Onchain, made invisible
description: Why MovenRun is onchain, and how embedded wallets, sponsored gas, and account abstraction make that power invisible — so anyone can play in seconds.
headings: Full control when you want it
keywords: onchain, made invisible movenrun is fully onchain — and you'd never know it unless you wanted to. that combination is the whole strategy: real ownership, zero friction, everyone welcome. why onchain at all because ownership only means something if it's real. when your territory, your deeds, and your $move live onchain under your keys, they're genuinely yours — portable, provable, and outside any single company's control. that's what turns a movement game into a movement network : an open economy players own, rather than a closed app they rent. why you never see it onchain has historically meant seed phrases, gas tokens, network switching, and signature prompts — a wall most people never climb. movenrun removes the wall: tap, move, own. the wallet, the gas, and the signatures are handled for you. embedded wallets. a secure wallet is created for you at sign-up — no seed phrase to write dow

title: The app
description: The MovenRun mobile experience: fast onboarding, live movement sessions, a rich territory map, and a game that feels native while settling value onchain.
keywords: the app the movenrun app is where the network meets the street: instant onboarding, live movement sessions, a rich territory map, and a game that feels native while settling value onchain. instant onboarding getting in takes a tap. an account and an embedded wallet are provisioned in the background, and the player is moving within seconds — no install friction, no seed phrase, no purchase. the crypto exists from the first second; it just never announces itself. movement sessions the session flow — start, move, finish, capture — is the core of the app. foreground gps records the route while distance, time, and pace stay glanceable, then the summary and capture moment turn the outing into territory. see movement & proof of activity . the territory map and game beyond sessions, the app is a full game: a living territory map of your holdings and their state, per-zone detail views, progre

title: Movement verification
description: How MovenRun proves real movement: signal validation, anti-spoofing checks, deterministic route proofs, deduplication, and an oracle that attests activity onchain.
keywords: movement verification ownership is only as trustworthy as the movement behind it. movenrun's verification layer turns a recorded route into a proof the whole economy can rely on. why it matters if territory is a real asset, the network can't reward movement it can't trust. verification is what keeps the map fair, the economy healthy, and ownership meaningful — it's the difference between a game of effort and a game of exploits. the proof-of-movement pipeline a recorded route becomes a signed proof that onchain contracts can act on. validate. the route is checked for physical plausibility — realistic speed, sane accuracy, continuous motion, sane duration. map. the path is resolved to the ordered set of zones it crossed (see territory computation ). reduce. the session is distilled into a compact, deterministic proof — a fingerprint of the movement, not a stored trail. attest. the oracle s

title: Territory computation
description: How routes become ownable zones: the H3 hexagonal grid explained simply, why hexagons, and how movement maps cleanly onto territory and districts.
headings: A single grid for the whole world
keywords: territory computation how a wandering gps route becomes crisp, ownable territory: one global hex grid, and a clean way to turn movement into map. a single grid for the whole world movenrun models the planet with h3 , an open hexagonal grid system that tiles the entire earth into cells. every location belongs to exactly one cell with a stable identifier, which gives the game a shared, precise definition of "a zone" — the same for every player, everywhere. that shared grid is what makes territory ownership consistent and composable. why hexagons even neighbors. every hexagon borders six others at equal distance, so adjacency — central to holding, spreading, and defending territory — behaves cleanly. natural fit to movement. routes curve; hexagons approximate the reach around a path far better than squares. clean aggregation. cells roll up into larger cells, which is how zones combine into

title: Protocol & contracts
description: The onchain protocol on Base: the token, zone-deed, challenge, season, vault, and governance contracts, and how verified movement drives them.
keywords: protocol & contracts movenrun's onchain protocol is a set of coordinated contracts on base that turn verified movement into ownership, economy, and governance. the protocol at a glance the protocol is the system of record for everything that matters: who owns which zones, how much $move exists and moves, how territory is contested, how seasons resolve, and how the community governs. verified movement, attested by the oracle, is the input that drives it. how the contracts relate: movement flows in through the oracle; ownership, economy, and governance flow out. the contracts the movenrun protocol contracts and their roles contract role $move token the network's asset. minted by verified movement within a capped schedule; used and burned across the game. zone deeds the ownership primitive — one onchain deed per map zone, held under the owner's keys, with provenance, yield, and defense.

title: Zone Deeds
description: Zone Deeds are the onchain ownership primitive: one deed per map zone, transferable, provable, with built-in defense, yield, and provenance.
headings: Owned by you, managed for you
keywords: zone deeds zone deeds are movenrun's ownership primitive — the onchain assets that make territory genuinely yours. one deed, one zone each captured zone maps to a single zone deed: a unique onchain asset identified by that zone's place on the global grid. a deed is held under the owner's keys and behaves like real property inside the game — you can hold it, strengthen it, defend it, and transfer it, and its history is recorded onchain. a deed binds a place on the map to an asset the owner controls. what a deed carries ownership & provenance. who holds it now, and the history of how it changed hands. defense. a strength that rises with active holding and fortification and is tested by challenges. yield & loyalty. active holders accrue in-game yield, with loyalty that rewards long-term stewardship. composability. deeds group into districts and city power, and interoperate with the

title: Token & economy
description: The $MOVE economy: how movement mints value, the utility that gives the token demand, the sinks that balance it, and the caps that keep it sustainable.
keywords: $move economy $move is the asset that powers movenrun. it's minted by movement, demanded by utility, and tightened by sinks — an economy engineered to reinforce itself. this page describes the $move economic design . it is not financial advice, an offer, or a promise of returns, and it quotes no price or yield figure. parameters are set and adjusted through governance and may evolve. see legal & disclaimers . the economic loop movement mints, utility demands, sinks burn — a loop built to hold together. how $move enters $move is minted by verified movement — real, proven activity, never a faucet. emission follows a capped, decaying schedule : issuance is highest early and steps down over time, so the network can bootstrap without inflating forever. per-period limits prevent any single account from dominating issuance. why $move is wanted demand comes from utility woven through the gam

title: Governance & the DAO
description: How MovenRun becomes community-owned: onchain voting, the treasury, a timelock-secured DAO, and a path from team stewardship to decentralized control.
keywords: governance & the dao movenrun is built to be owned by the people who move on it. governance is onchain, timelock-secured, and progressively decentralized. how governance works propose, vote, wait, execute — with a timelock protecting every change. propose. stakeholders put forward changes — to parameters, the treasury, or the protocol itself. vote. voting power is based on a governance snapshot, so influence can't be gamed by shuffling tokens. timelock. passed proposals wait through a delay, giving the community time to review before anything takes effect. execute. after the delay, the change executes onchain, transparently. the community treasury the network's vault holds a community treasury that governance directs — funding growth, rewards, partnerships, and the ongoing development of the network. because it's onchain and timelock-secured, allocation is transparent and accountable

title: Security
description: How MovenRun protects users and the protocol: authentication, anti-abuse, role-based contract permissions, a timelock on privileged actions, and defense in depth.
keywords: security movenrun protects two things at once: the users who play, and the protocol that holds their ownership. security is designed in at every layer. movement integrity the first line of defense is proving movement is real. plausibility checks, deterministic proofs, and deduplication keep spoofed or replayed activity out of the economy, and the verification layer is designed to keep hardening as the network grows. see movement verification . account and asset safety user-controlled keys. deeds and $move are held under keys the user controls, even when the wallet is managed invisibly. authenticated actions. onchain operations are authorized by the user's wallet, so no one can act on their assets for them. self-custody available. players can export or connect their own wallet and take full control at any time. protocol security role-based permissions. privileged contract actions are gate

title: Roadmap
description: The MovenRun roadmap: from the movement beta to city-scale competition, onchain ownership on Base, and progressive decentralization of the network.
keywords: roadmap movenrun is built in the right order: prove the movement loop, turn the map into a contest, bring ownership fully onchain, and hand the network to its community. from a movement beta to a community-owned onchain network. phase 1 — movement & capture the foundation: fast onboarding, reliable movement sessions, verifiable proof of activity, and the capture loop that turns routes into territory. this phase proves the core experience feels great and that movement maps cleanly onto an ownable map. unlocks: sessions, route summaries, streaks, the territory map, and the first taste of owning the ground you cover. phase 2 — clubs & city competition the map becomes a contest. clubs, crew missions, district control, city wars, and seasons turn individual holdings into team strategy and citywide rivalry, with the social depth that keeps a network alive. unlocks: clubs and leaderboar

title: Frequently asked questions
description: Straight answers about MovenRun: what it is, how ownership works, whether you need crypto experience, how movement is verified, and how the economy works.
headings: Do I really own my territory?
Do I need to know anything about crypto?
Do I have to buy anything to start?
How does MovenRun know I actually moved?
What happens to my location data?
keywords: frequently asked questions straight answers about what movenrun is and how it works. what is movenrun? an onchain movement network on base. you move in the real world, capture the zones you cross on a global hex map, and own that territory as onchain assets called zone deeds . a native token, $move , powers the economy, and the network is designed to be community-governed. do i really own my territory? yes. zone deeds are onchain assets held under keys you control — not a row in a database the operator can revoke. you can hold, defend, and transfer them, and take full self-custody whenever you want. do i need to know anything about crypto? no. movenrun creates a secure wallet for you automatically, covers gas fees, and hides all the blockchain machinery behind an ordinary game. you get real ownership without ever managing keys or buying a token first. see onchain, made invisible . do i h

title: Glossary
description: Definitions for the MovenRun world: zones, capture, fortify, districts, Zone Deeds, $MOVE, proof of movement, oracle, H3, and more.
keywords: glossary the language of the movenrun world, defined once. movement session a recorded period of walking, running, or cycling — the fundamental action in movenrun. proof of movement a verifiable, deterministic proof that a session really happened, attested by the oracle so onchain contracts can act on it. zone one hexagonal cell of the world map — the atom of territory. every location belongs to exactly one zone. territory the set of zones a player owns and maintains. capture claiming the zones a verified route crossed, turning them into owned territory. zone deed the onchain asset representing ownership of a zone — one deed per zone, held under the owner's keys, carrying provenance, defense, and yield. defense a zone's resistance to being taken; it rises with active holding and fortification and is tested by challenges. fortify an action that strengthens a zone you hold. challenge the o

title: Risk factors
description: A candid look at the risks in building an onchain movement network — technical, economic, and regulatory — and how MovenRun's design mitigates them.
keywords: risk factors building an onchain movement network is ambitious, and honesty about the risks is part of building it well. here are the real ones — and how movenrun's design addresses them. movement verification risk: location can be faked, and an ownership economy built on movement must resist spoofing. approach: layered verification — plausibility checks, deterministic proofs, deduplication, and an oracle that attests activity onchain — designed to keep hardening as the network grows. verification integrity is treated as foundational. see movement verification . economic balance risk: movement economies have failed when incentives outran utility. approach: movenrun is designed utility-first — capped, decaying issuance; sinks and burns before incentives; ownership and demand woven through the game — so value has reasons to persist beyond emissions. parameters are governed transparently on

title: Legal & disclaimers
description: Important notices: MovenRun is a development-stage product, this documentation is informational and forward-looking, and nothing here is financial advice.
keywords: legal & disclaimers plain, important notices about this documentation and the movenrun product. development-stage product movenrun is under active development. this documentation describes the network's intended design and is forward-looking : features, parameters, mechanics, and timelines may change, and described functionality may not yet be available. nothing here should be read as a guarantee that any specific feature will ship in any particular form or timeframe. not financial advice nothing in this documentation is financial, investment, legal, or tax advice, an offer or solicitation, or a recommendation to buy, sell, or hold any asset. movenrun makes no promise of profit, income, yield, or financial return of any kind. descriptions of the $move economy and zone deeds are descriptions of product and protocol design , not offers or projections, and quote no price. digital assets
